---
title: "map_rc"
author: "Franklin Qian"
date: "July 3, 2017"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
require("knitr")
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/Dropbox/rent_control/Franklin/rent-control")
rm(list=ls())
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:


## Load Packages
```{r eval=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
library(devtools) 
library(ggplot2)
library(ggmap)
library(data.table)
```

## Load Data
```{r}
parcels <- read.table("data/infutor/parcels_formap.txt", header = TRUE)
setDT(parcels)
parcels[, rc := ifelse(treat=="Treat" & use_code==3, 1, 2)]
parcels[use_code!=3, rc := 3]
parcels[, rc := as.factor(rc)]
parcels[, opacity := ifelse(rc!=3, 1, 0.05)]
parcels[rc==1, opacity := 0.75]
#parcels[, pointsize := ifelse(rc==2, I(1), I(0.5))]
```

```{r}
theme_set(theme_bw(16))
sfmap <- qmplot(lon_assessor_sr, lat_assessor_sr, data = parcels[rc==3,], colour = rc, size = I(0.5), alpha = opacity, legend = "topleft")+
  geom_point(data=parcels[rc==1,], aes(x = lon_assessor_sr, y = lat_assessor_sr, color = rc, alpha=opacity), size=I(0.5))+
  geom_point(data=parcels[rc==2,], aes(x = lon_assessor_sr, y = lat_assessor_sr, color = rc, alpha=opacity), size=I(1))+
  guides(colour=guide_legend(title=NULL, override.aes = list(size=2)),
         alpha=FALSE,
         size=FALSE)+ 
  scale_colour_manual(values=c("#CC79A7", "#009E73","#999999"),
                      labels=c("Rent-Contolled Sample", "Uncontrolled Sample", "Others"))+
  theme(legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10)) 
sfmap
ggsave("output/map_rc.pdf", sfmap, width = 9, height = 9)
```
